channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: One Fell Swoop Circus to perform new show at South Side Festival in Frankston
item.description: One Fell Swoop Circus is set to bring its latest production to the South Side Festival in Frankston. The acrobatic troupe will showcase a brand new show as part of the local community event, promising...

item.title: Alberta family highlights impact of organ and tissue donation after tragic loss
item.description: An Alberta mother is sharing her son’s story to raise awareness about organ and tissue donation, emphasizing how tragedy can lead to life-saving outcomes for others. Carrie Balkwill spoke about her so...

item.title: Senior cardiologist advocates improved early heart disease screening and prevention
item.description: Dr Stephen Fenton, a 74-year-old senior cardiologist, has spent over 40 years in the field and is now focusing on preventing heart attacks before they occur. Motivated by personal experiences, includi...

item.title: Philippines Leads ASEAN Effort for Unified Climate Position at COP31
item.description: The Philippine government is actively coordinating with ASEAN member states to form a strong, unified stance on climate-related responsibilities before the UN Climate Change Conference (COP31) schedul...

item.title: Three people drown in river during baptism ceremony in Mpumalanga
item.description: In a tragic incident in Mpumalanga, three individuals lost their lives on Sunday morning while participating in a church baptism ceremony at the Wellas River behind Extension 22 in Embalenhle near Sec...

item.title: US veteran warns of sophisticated social media investment scams after major financial loss
item.description: A disabled United States veteran, Craig Wilkinson, has spoken publicly after losing more than $170,000 in a sophisticated social media investment scam, highlighting the growing threat of online financ...

item.title: Debates over the role of culture and politics on Anzac Day
item.description: Anzac Day, a solemn occasion in Australia and New Zealand to honour military service and sacrifice, is increasingly becoming a stage for cultural and political debates. The article discusses how onlin...

item.title: Court in Nairobi sentences Somali man for recruiting and radicalising a minor into Al-Shabaab
item.description: A Nairobi court has sentenced a Somali national, Abdikarim Hassanow Hassan, to prison after finding him guilty of multiple terrorism-related offences linked to Al-Shabaab. The ruling was delivered by ...

item.title: Four privately‑owned clubs promoted to Nigeria Premier Football League for 2026/27 season
item.description: Four privately‑owned clubs have been announced as the newly promoted teams to the Nigeria Premier Football League (NPFL) for the 2026/27 season, reflecting a growing shift towards private investment i...

item.title: Government Plans to Introduce Age Restrictions on Social Media Use
item.description: The government is considering measures to regulate access to social media platforms, with a focus on introducing age limits or restrictions. This initiative aims to address growing concerns over the i...

item.title: Christine McGuinness discusses her sexuality and dating life post-divorce
item.description: Three years after her divorce from television presenter Paddy McGuinness, Christine McGuinness has spoken openly about her sexuality and dating experiences. In a recent interview on the LGBTQ+ podcast...

item.title: A Closer Look at 'The Third Man': A Classic Tale of Lies, Shadows, and Intrigue
item.description: Carol Reed's 'The Third Man', a black-and-white film set in post-World War II Vienna, has become one of the most memorable films in cinematic history. Based on Graham Greene's novella, the film introd...

item.title: Edmonton Tim Hortons Smile Cookie Campaign Supports Police Youth Programs
item.description: The annual Tim Hortons Smile Cookie campaign has officially begun in Edmonton, raising funds for the Edmonton Police Foundation (EPF). The campaign kicked off at the Edmonton Convention Centre with a ...

item.title: Ongoing Tensions in the Middle East as of April 28, 2026
item.description: As of April 28, 2026, tensions in the Middle East continue to escalate. Key hotspots include critical maritime routes such as the Strait of Hormuz near Musandam, Oman, which remains a point of strateg...

item.title: Ramaphosa attends Eswatini’s Ruby Jubilee celebrations for King Mswati III
item.description: President Cyril Ramaphosa attended national celebrations in the Kingdom of Eswatini to mark two significant milestones for King Mswati III: the 40th anniversary of his coronation and his 58th birthday...

item.title: Remi Tinubu launches community food bank initiative in Borno to tackle malnutrition
item.description: Nigeria First Lady, Senator Oluremi Tinubu, visit Borno State where she officially launch the National Community Food Bank Programme for the North-East region. The programme, wey she flag off for Maid...

item.title: Diesel and kerosene prices drop further while gasoline slightly increases for third straight week
item.description: Oil companies in the Philippines implemented another round of fuel price adjustments on April 28, marking the third consecutive week of rollbacks for diesel and kerosene after earlier increases linked...

item.title: Chernobyl Disaster: Key Facts on the 40th Anniversary
item.description: On April 26, 2026, Ukraine marks the 40th anniversary of the catastrophic explosion at the Chernobyl nuclear power plant, which remains the worst civilian nuclear disaster in history. The anniversary ...

item.title: King Charles Visits US to Strengthen Diplomatic Ties Amid Tensions
item.description: King Charles III don land Washington for him state visit wey dey meant to celebrate 250 years since US independence from Britain. Him meet former President Donald Trump for White House as dem try smoo...

item.title: Bec Judd enjoys Palm Springs and Coachella with friends and family
